2606.06080.title: On Advantage Estimates for Max@K Policy Gradients
2606.06080.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards is widely used for post-training reasoning models, but sparse outcome rewards make exploration difficult. A complementary approach is to optimize inference-time objectives such as pass@K and max@K directly, yet existing policy-gradient estimators for these objectives use different signals, baselines, and normalizations, making their relationships unclear. We study this issue through baseline design and advantage centering. Starting from the advantage estimator of a leading method in the field, we show that it is policy-gradient unbiased but yields a non-centered advantage. We then introduce a Leave-Two-Out baseline that preserves policy-gradient unbiasedness while making realized batch advantages exactly centered. The resulting method, MaxPO, has an efficient quadratic-time implementation and integrates naturally into group-based RL for LLM post-training. We further derive the canonical finite-batch advantage for max@K, providing a unified view of existing advantage estimators. Empirically, we verify that the L2O baseline reduces gradient variance and outperforms non-centered alternatives.
2606.06080.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習與可驗證獎勵廣泛應用於訓練後推理模型，但稀疏的結果獎勵使得探索變得困難。 一種補充的方法是直接優化推理時間目標，如 pass@K 和 max@K，然而現有的這些目標的策略梯度估計器使用不同的信號、基準和正規化，導致它們之間的關係不明確。 我們通過基準設計和優勢中心化來研究這個問題。 從該領域一個領先方法的優勢估計器開始，我們展示了它是無偏的策略梯度，但產生了非中心化的優勢。 我們隨後引入了一個 Leave-Two-Out 基準，該基準在保持策略梯度無偏的同時，使得實現的批量優勢恰好中心化。 最終的方法 MaxPO 具有高效的二次時間實現，並自然整合到基於群體的強化學習中，用於 LLM 訓練後。 我們進一步推導了 max@K 的經典有限批量優勢，提供了現有優勢估計器的統一視角。 實證上，我們驗證了 L2O 基準減少了梯度方差，並且優於非中心化的替代方案。
2606.06080.author: Shota Takashiro et.al.
2606.06080.authors: Shota Takashiro, Soichiro Nishimori, Paavo Parmas, Yongmin Kim, Kohsei Matsutani, Gouki Minegishi, Yusuke Iwasawa, Takeshi Kojima, Yutaka Matsuo
